description: Phase 3 - Create packages/server-core with Fastify-based HTTP layer and convert QuantMail identity service routes to use it. Includes Fastify app factory with helmet, cors, rate-limit (Redis-backed), pino logger, request-id, Zod validation via @fastify/type-provider-zod, error handler, graceful shutdown. Also includes auth plugin that validates JWTs via jose, health/readiness endpoints, and a converted QuantMail identity API.
steps: Step 1: Create packages/server-core directory with package.json. Dependencies: fastify, @fastify/helmet, @fastify/cors, @fastify/rate-limit, @fastify/cookie, fastify-plugin, zod, @fastify/type-provider-zod, pino, pino-pretty (dev), ioredis. Dev deps: @types/node, vitest. Add workspace deps: @quant/auth (workspace:*), @quant/common (workspace:*). Add tsconfig.json extending root. Add vitest.config.ts.

Step 2: Create packages/server-core/src/app.ts - Export createApp(config: AppConfig) function that: creates Fastify instance with logger (pino), registers @fastify/helmet, registers @fastify/cors with configurable origins, registers @fastify/rate-limit with Redis store (IORedis) or in-memory fallback, adds request-id decorator (crypto.randomUUID), adds Zod type provider via withTypeProvider<ZodTypeProvider>(), registers error handler plugin, registers graceful shutdown hooks (SIGTERM, SIGINT).

Step 3: Create packages/server-core/src/plugins/error-handler.ts - Fastify plugin that sets a custom error handler: maps Zod validation errors to 400 with details, maps known AppError instances to their status code, maps unknown errors to 500 with generic message in production (full in dev). Returns standard { success, error: { code, message, statusCode, details } } format.

Step 4: Create packages/server-core/src/plugins/auth.ts - Fastify plugin that decorates request with 'auth' property (AuthContext from @quant/auth types). Provides a preHandler hook factory: requireAuth(options?: { scopes?: string[] }) that extracts Bearer token, verifies with jose (using the same secret/verification as the new token-service), and populates request.auth. If invalid, returns 401. If missing scopes, returns 403.

Step 5: Create packages/server-core/src/plugins/health.ts - Fastify plugin that registers GET /healthz (always returns { status: 'ok' }) and GET /readyz (checks Redis connectivity if configured, returns 200 or 503).

Step 6: Create packages/server-core/src/types.ts - Export AppConfig interface { port, host, logLevel, corsOrigins, rateLimitMax, rateLimitWindow, redisUrl?, jwtSecret, jwtIssuer, jwtAudience, env }. Export AuthenticatedRequest type extending FastifyRequest with auth property.
Step 7: Create packages/server-core/src/index.ts - barrel export: createApp, error handler, auth plugin, health plugin, types, AppConfig.

Step 8: Create packages/server-core/src/__tests__/app.test.ts - Tests using Fastify's inject() method (no actual server binding needed): app starts and responds to /healthz with 200, unknown routes return 404 with standard error format, Zod validation errors return 400 with field details, auth plugin rejects requests without Bearer token, auth plugin accepts valid JWT.
Step 9: Update root tsconfig.json to add path alias @quant/server-core -> packages/server-core/src/index.ts and add reference.

Step 10: Create apps/quantmail/api-v2/ directory for the Fastify-based identity service. Create apps/quantmail/api-v2/app.ts that imports createApp from @quant/server-core, configures it for QuantMail, and registers route plugins.

Step 11: Create apps/quantmail/api-v2/routes/auth.ts - Fastify route plugin with Zod-validated routes: POST /auth/register (Zod schema: email, username, displayName, password with min 8 chars, acceptedTerms), POST /auth/login (email/username + password), POST /auth/token/refresh (refreshToken body), POST /auth/logout. Each route handler calls the appropriate service methods from @quant/auth. Use request.server.inject pattern for testability.

Step 12: Create apps/quantmail/api-v2/routes/oauth.ts - Fastify route plugin: GET /oauth/authorize, POST /oauth/token, POST /oauth/revoke, GET /.well-known/jwks.json (returns JWKS from token service). Zod schemas for all request bodies.
Step 13: Create apps/quantmail/api-v2/routes/wellknown.ts - GET /.well-known/openid-configuration returning standard OIDC discovery metadata pointing to the QuantMail endpoints.

Step 14: Create apps/quantmail/api-v2/__tests__/auth-routes.test.ts - Integration tests using app.inject(): successful registration returns 201 with tokens, login with correct credentials returns tokens, login with wrong password returns 401, refresh token returns new token pair, logout invalidates session, register with invalid email returns 400 validation error.
Step 15: Update apps/quantmail/package.json to add dependency on @quant/server-core (workspace:*) and add fastify/zod/ioredis deps.

Step 16: Create apps/quantmail/Dockerfile - Multi-stage build: Stage 1 (builder) uses node:22-alpine, installs pnpm, copies workspace, runs pnpm install --frozen-lockfile, runs tsc. Stage 2 (runner) uses node:22-alpine (distroless-style: non-root user, minimal packages), copies built output, sets NODE_ENV=production, exposes port 3001, CMD node dist/api-v2/app.js. (Note: Dockerfile is for documentation/CI - we cannot run docker build in this env.)
Step 17: Run pnpm install, then run pnpm typecheck to verify TypeScript compiles across all packages.
Step 18: Run tests for server-core and quantmail api-v2 to verify everything passes.
acceptance_criteria: packages/server-core exists with createApp() factory function
Fastify instance includes helmet, cors, rate-limit, pino logger, request-id
Zod type provider is configured for request validation
Auth plugin validates JWTs via jose and populates request.auth
Error handler returns standard error format for validation errors (400) and unknown errors (500)
apps/quantmail/api-v2/ contains Fastify-based identity routes with Zod validation
At least POST /auth/register, POST /auth/login, POST /auth/token/refresh are implemented
GET /.well-known/jwks.json returns a valid JWKS document
Integration tests pass using Fastify inject()
TypeScript compiles cleanly across all workspace packages
verification: Run: cd packages/server-core && npx vitest run (all server-core tests pass)
Run: cd apps/quantmail && npx vitest run (all quantmail tests pass)
Run: pnpm typecheck (TypeScript compiles across workspace)
findings: 1. The npm package is named 'fastify-type-provider-zod' (not @fastify/type-provider-zod). 2. In Fastify 5, decorateRequest requires a value matching the type declaration - using 'undefined as unknown as T' pattern. 3. Routes must be registered before app.ready() is called. 4. Pre-existing TypeScript errors exist in apps/quantmail/src/ TSX files (numeric literal parse errors) unrelated to this feature. 5. lint-staged with prettier runs successfully on commit.
